items.title: Medtronic confirms breach after hackers claim 9 million records theft
items.summary: Medical device giant Medtronic disclosed last week that hackers breached its network and accessed data in "certain corporate IT systems." [...]

items.title: 20-Year-Old Malware Rewrites History of Cyber Sabotage
items.summary: Researchers have uncovered a malware framework dubbed "fast16" that predates Stuxnet by 5 years.

items.title: Money launderer linked to $230M crypto heist gets 70 months in prison
items.summary: ​22-year-old Evan Tangeman of Newport Beach, California, was sentenced to 70 months in prison for laundering funds stolen in a massive $230 million cryptocurrency heist. [...]

items.title: Deepfake Voice Attacks are Outpacing Defenses: What Security Leaders Should Know
items.summary: Three seconds of audio is all it takes to clone a voice for fraud. Adaptive Security shows how deepfake calls trick employees into sending real money—and why most defenses don't catch them. [...]

items.title: Parsing Agentic Offensive Security's Existential Threat
items.summary: Some fear frontier LLMs like Claude Mythos and Anthropic's GPT-5.5 will lead to cybersecurity annihilation. Ari Herbert-Voss notes this could be an opportunity.

items.title: Microsoft says Outlook.com outage is causing sign‑in failures
items.summary: Microsoft is investigating an ongoing Outlook.com outage that is causing intermittent signing issues and preventing customers from accessing their mailboxes. [...]

items.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
items.summary: A pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group called PhantomCore has been attributed to attacks actively targeting servers running TrueConf video conferencing software in Russia since September 2025. That's according to a report published by Positive…

items.title: Researchers Uncover 73 Fake VS Code Extensions Delivering GlassWorm v2 Malware
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged dozens of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions on the Open VSX repository that are linked to a persistent information-stealing campaign dubbed GlassWorm. The cluster of 73 extensions has…

items.title: American utility firm Itron discloses breach of internal IT network
items.summary: Itron, Inc. has disclosed, via an 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party accessed certain internal systems. [...]

items.title: Microsoft rolls out revamped Windows Insider Program
items.summary: Microsoft says it's rolling out a revamped Windows Insider Program experience as part of the broader plans to address performance and reliability concerns affecting Windows 11. [...]

items.title: Threat actor uses Microsoft Teams to deploy new “Snow” malware
items.summary: A threat group tracked as UNC6692 uses social engineering to deploy a new, custom malware suite named 'Snow' which includes a browser extension, a tunneler, and a backdoor. [...]

items.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment centrifuges. According to a new report…

items.title: ADT confirms data breach after ShinyHunters leak threat
items.summary: Home security giant ADT has confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]

items.title: Helping Romance Scam Victims Requires a Proactive, Empathic Approach
items.summary: People targeted by confidence schemes find getting help is a lonely road. Experts want law enforcement, financial, and government institutions to work together and protect them.

items.title: US Busts Myanmar Ring Targeting US Citizens in Financial Fraud
items.summary: Some 29 people were charged, including a Cambodian senator, and authorities seized more than 500 Web domains tied to fake investment sites.

items.title: Glasswing Secured the Code. The Rest of Your Stack Is Still on You
items.summary: Forgotten integrations, shadow IT, SaaS, and now shadow AI and agents are everywhere, and attackers don't need sophisticated AI models to take advantage.

items.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
items.summary: In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.

items.title: North Korea's Lazarus Targets macOS Users via ClickFix
items.summary: Lazarus continues leveraging ClickFix for initial access and data theft: in this case, against Mac-centric organizations and their high-value leaders.
